title: What Time's Throw In? – Irish Sports TV Schedule Tool
overview: A single-page, self-contained HTML/CSS/JS web tool that displays upcoming Irish sports fixture start times and their broadcast channels (TV or streaming service). The tool targets Ireland's most popular sports—rugby, soccer, GAA (hurling & football), golf, and horse racing—with a focus on weekend fixtures as the primary viewing window. All data is embedded as static sample fixtures with no server-side dependencies, external databases, or authentication. The page must be responsive, work on desktop and mobile, and include clear on-page usage instructions.
objectives: Provide a single, easy-to-use page showing upcoming Irish sports fixtures with start times and broadcast channels
Cover the five core Irish-interest sports: rugby, soccer, GAA (hurling & football), golf, and horse racing
Highlight weekend (Saturday & Sunday) fixtures as the primary viewing window
Deliver a fully self-contained static HTML file with inline CSS and JS, zero external dependencies
Ensure responsive design for both desktop and mobile devices
Include clear on-page usage instructions so first-time visitors can immediately understand the tool

user_stories.i_want: to see a list of upcoming sports fixtures with start times and TV channels
user_stories.so_that: I can quickly find out when and where to watch my favourite sports
acceptance_criteria: Each fixture entry displays: sport type, event/match name, date/time, and TV channel or streaming service
All times are displayed in Irish Standard Time (IST/GMT)
At least one sample fixture exists for each of the five core sports

user_stories.i_want: to filter fixtures by sport type
user_stories.so_that: I can focus on the sports I care about most
acceptance_criteria: Filter controls are visible on the page for each of the five sports categories
Selecting a sport filter shows only fixtures for that sport
A way to clear filters and show all fixtures is available
Filters work entirely client-side with no page reload

user_stories.i_want: weekend fixtures to be prominently displayed or default-filtered
user_stories.so_that: I can immediately see what's on this Saturday and Sunday without extra clicks
acceptance_criteria: Weekend (Saturday and Sunday) fixtures are displayed by default or prominently highlighted
A toggle or filter allows showing weekday fixtures as well
Weekend fixtures are visually distinguishable from weekday fixtures

user_stories.i_want: the page to be responsive and usable on my phone
user_stories.so_that: I can check fixtures on the go
acceptance_criteria: The layout adapts to screen widths from 320px to 1920px+
All interactive elements (filters, buttons) are touch-friendly (minimum 44px tap target)
Text is readable without horizontal scrolling on mobile devices
No content is clipped or hidden on small screens

user_stories.i_want: to see clear usage instructions on the page
user_stories.so_that: I understand how to use the tool without any external documentation
acceptance_criteria: Usage instructions are visible on the page without requiring user action
Instructions explain how to filter by sport and view weekend fixtures
Instructions are concise and do not dominate the page layout

user_stories.i_want: the tool to be a single self-contained HTML file with no external dependencies
user_stories.so_that: I can deploy it by simply placing the file at a URL path on any static host
acceptance_criteria: The entire tool is contained in one .html file
All CSS is inline or in a <style> block within the file
All JavaScript is inline or in a <script> block within the file
No external CDN links, fonts, stylesheets, or script references
The file opens correctly when loaded directly in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge

user_stories.i_want: to see which TV channel or streaming service is broadcasting each event
user_stories.so_that: I know where to tune in or which app to open
acceptance_criteria: Each fixture clearly displays the broadcast channel or streaming service name
Channel names include relevant Irish broadcasters (e.g., RTÉ, TG4, Virgin Media, Sky Sports, TNT Sports, Premier Sports, GAAGO, Racing TV)
The channel information is visually prominent within each fixture entry

user_stories.i_want: to filter or sort fixtures by date
user_stories.so_that: I can plan my viewing for a specific day
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures can be viewed grouped or sorted by date
The current or next upcoming date is highlighted or prioritised

constraints: Must be a single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JS—no external dependencies of any kind
No external database, authentication, or server-side runtime required
Data is embedded/static—no live API integrations in v1.0
Must function as a static file served at a path URL
Target modern browsers only: latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge
Agent budget capped at $0.75 USD; delivery timeout of 600 seconds
All displayed times in Irish Standard Time (IST/GMT)

assumptions.assumption: The scope of 'top sports' is limited to five categories
assumptions.default: Rugby, soccer, GAA (hurling & football), golf, and horse racing

assumptions.assumption: No user accounts or personalisation features are needed
assumptions.default: All visitors see the same schedule view with client-side filtering only

assumptions.assumption: Weekend is defined as Saturday and Sunday
assumptions.default: Fixtures shown default-filter to Saturday and Sunday

assumptions.assumption: Live data feeds or API integrations are not required for v1.0
assumptions.default: Sport schedule data will be hardcoded as sample/representative fixtures within the HTML file

assumptions.assumption: The tool targets modern browsers only, not legacy
assumptions.default: Latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge

assumptions.assumption: Time zone for all displayed times is Irish Standard Time
Sample fixture data should represent realistic upcoming Irish sports events
assumptions.default: Use representative fixture names, realistic kick-off times, and actual Irish broadcaster names
